title: Scientists Built a Chip That Computes With Light, Not Electricity
script: Every chip in your phone runs on electrons. Electrons generate heat, waste energy, and hit a speed wall. Scientists at Monash University just built a chip that computes using light instead. It exploits a property of atomically thin materials called the 'valley degree of freedom.' The field is called valleytronics. If it scales, it could slash computing energy use by 90 percent and replace silicon entirely.
